purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.body:
## Purpose

Use this pack only when the initialized project is explicitly a game or game-adjacent interactive simulation.

This pack adds game-development constraints without making any specific engine the default.

recommended_pairing.title: Recommended Pairing
recommended_pairing.body:
## Recommended Pairing

Use `gamedev` together with `code` for game software projects.

The `code` pack remains the generic programming baseline. This pack adds domain-specific concerns such as loops, frames, input, assets, deterministic state, simulation, content pipelines, build exports, profiling, and player-facing validation.

gamedev_defaults.title: Gamedev Defaults
gamedev_defaults.body:
## Gamedev Defaults

- Keep simulation state and presentation glue separate when the engine allows it.
- Make lifecycle boundaries explicit: boot, load, update, pause, save, unload, and shutdown.
- Treat frame budget, allocation behavior, asset loading, and input latency as first-class constraints.
- Prefer engine-native systems and proven game architecture patterns before custom infrastructure.
- Keep data contracts explicit for content, save data, procedural generation, and editor/export tooling.
- Add diagnostics that explain runtime state without flooding the main loop.

verification.title: Verification
verification.body:
## Verification

Use the strongest practical game-project gate available:

- unit or domain tests,
- content validation,
- asset import/export checks,
- build/export smoke test,
- frame-time or memory profiling,
- replay or deterministic fixture checks,
- manual playtest focused on the changed loop.

If the project has an engine-specific test runner or export command, prefer that command over generic manual checks.
